newsSinceJun6: Stock jumped +9.52% on June 9 (near Taiwan's 10% limit-up). Drivers: May 2026 revenue NT$6.762B (+13.0% YoY) released around June 8-9; a flow-driven LEO-satellite narrative after Starlink's prominence during the Iran network shutdown; and continued AI-server order chatter (50+ layer server boards, expected entry into US AI mainboard supply chain). Capex hike to ~NT$9B and Thailand/Taiwan expansion were already known from late May; analyst median target was raised 270->285 in late May, and today's 276 close has nearly consumed it. Judgment: today's move is predominantly theme/flow-driven, not new fundamental evidence - May revenue +13% YoY does not justify a near limit-up day on a stock already up ~192% YTD.

sources.label: CMoney research note - Compeq up on AI server + LEO satellite theme rebound, technical strength (Jun 2026)
MoneyDJ - Compeq May 2026 revenue NT$6.762B, +13.02% YoY
Business Today - Compeq's two aces (AI server boards, SpaceX/LEO satellite boards); analyst median target raised to 285
Economic Daily (UDN Money) - Compeq capex increase and 2026 profit roadmap

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha lens, a near limit-up +9.5% day driven by a satellite-news narrative and momentum flows - on top of +192% YTD and a price that has already consumed the freshly raised 285 median analyst target - compresses what little hidden upside remained. Yesterday's verdict was already down; today's flow-driven spike is genuinely new price action that worsens the crowding setup further.
verdicts.bull: Global #1 in HDI PCB (Prismark 2026), dual engines of AI server boards (50+ layers, expected US AI mainboard supply-chain entry) and LEO satellite boards for Starlink/Kuiper; capex raised to ~NT$9B with Thailand/Taiwan expansion supports multi-year growth.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentals are intact and improving (May revenue +13% YoY, 1Q26 net income +14% YoY), but the pace of fundamental delivery now lags the pace of the rerating; fair-value case rests on H2 satellite and datacenter revenue actually landing.
verdicts.bear: +192% YTD with a near limit-up day on essentially recycled news is classic late-stage theme chasing; +13% YoY revenue growth is modest versus the multiple, retail/momentum ownership is rising, and any AI-server or Starlink order air-pocket would hit a crowded register hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Confirmed US AI mainboard/AI server design-win revenue and H2 2026 satellite + datacenter contribution at the July 31 earnings report.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Flow reversal in a crowded, fully-rerated name - monthly revenue growth (~13% YoY) failing to validate the ~192% YTD rerating, or LEO satellite order timing slipping.
